rounds.clue: The birthplace of jazz, this city sits where the Mississippi River meets the Gulf of Mexico.
This iconic arch stands 630 feet tall on the western bank of the Mississippi River, symbolizing the gateway to the West.
Home to the world's largest collection of geysers, including Old Faithful, this park sits atop a massive supervolcano.
This Florida county is home to the most visited theme park destination on Earth.
This mountain capital city sits at 5,280 feet above sea level — exactly one mile high.
Known as the Windy City, this Great Lakes metropolis is famous for its deep-dish pizza and stunning architecture.
The largest national park in the US, this Alaskan wilderness spans over 13 million acres.
Carved into the granite face of the Black Hills, four presidents' faces overlook South Dakota.
This city on Puget Sound is the birthplace of Starbucks, Amazon, and grunge music.
The most populous county in Texas, home to Houston's Space Center and energy industry.
The birthplace of Elvis Presley and home to Graceland, this Tennessee city sits on the Mississippi River.
Rising 555 feet above the National Mall, this obelisk is the world's tallest stone structure.
The Grand Canyon was carved by the Colorado River over millions of years; it's a mile deep and 277 miles long.
The most populous county in the US, home to Los Angeles and Hollywood.
This small Vermont city is the least populous state capital in the US, home to just 8,000 people.
This California park in the Sierra Nevada is home to Half Dome, El Capitan, and Yosemite Falls.
This Georgia city hosted the 1996 Summer Olympics and is headquarters to Coca-Cola and CNN.
Alaska's capital city is accessible only by boat or plane — no roads connect it to the rest of the state.
This copper statue given by France stands on Liberty Island in New York Harbor.
Illinois' most populous county encompasses Chicago and its inner suburbs.
Spanning the Golden Gate strait, this iconic suspension bridge in San Francisco is painted International Orange.
Arizona's most populous county contains Phoenix, Scottsdale, and Tempe.
Nicknamed the City of Brotherly Love, it's home to the Liberty Bell and Independence Hall.
Connecticut's state capital along the Connecticut River was once the center of the American insurance industry.
Utah's smallest national park is packed with over 2,000 natural sandstone arches, including Delicate Arch.
This South Dakota capital sits on the Missouri River and has a population of just over 14,000.
This Montana park is called the Crown of the Continent and has over 700 miles of trails through pristine wilderness.
Abraham Lincoln sits enthroned at the west end of the National Mall in Washington, DC.
This Massachusetts city is home to the Red Sox, Harvard, and MIT.
This California county on the Pacific coast is home to San Diego, the nation's eighth largest city.
Home to Disneyland and Anaheim, this Southern California county is the second most populous in the state.
The capital of New Mexico is the oldest state capital city in the US, founded by the Spanish in 1610.
Sin City sits in the Mojave Desert and is famous for its casinos and the Strip.
The park with the most visitors in the US straddles the Tennessee-North Carolina border in the Blue Ridge Mountains.
rounds.answer: Great Smoky Mountains National Park, TN/NC

rounds.clue: This 102-story Art Deco skyscraper on Fifth Avenue was the world's tallest building from 1931 to 1970.
The Music City, home to the Grand Ole Opry and country music's biggest stars.
Atop Queen Anne Hill in Seattle, this 605-foot tower was built for the 1962 World's Fair.
Utah's first national park is famous for its towering red cliffs and the Virgin River Narrows.
This Florida county is the most populous in the state, home to Miami and its beaches.
This Wyoming capital is nicknamed the Magic City of the Plains and is the least populous state capital.
Named for its pink-red hoodoo rock spires, this Utah park sits on the Paunsaugunt Plateau.
This Texas city is the largest in the US by area and home to NASA's Johnson Space Center.
Idaho's capital city sits on the Boise River at the foot of the Rocky Mountains.
Built in the Nevada desert during the Great Depression, this concrete arch-gravity dam on the Colorado River was the world's tallest when completed.
The most populous county in Texas encompasses Dallas and many of its suburbs.
This cracked iron bell in Philadelphia rang to summon lawmakers to hear the first public reading of the Declaration of Independence.
This Nevada county in the Mojave Desert is home to Las Vegas and over 2 million people.
Nicknamed the City of Angels, it's the entertainment capital of the world.
The capital of Maryland sits on the Severn River and is home to the US Naval Academy.
This Colorado park offers 14,000-foot peaks, alpine meadows, and the highest paved road in the US.
This Kentucky capital is located on the Kentucky River and is famous for bourbon whiskey production.
The rainiest national park in the Lower 48, this Washington park on the Olympic Peninsula features temperate rainforests.
Built in 1718, this Spanish mission in San Antonio is famous for the 1836 battle where Davy Crockett fell.
This Arizona city is the most populous state capital in the US and gets over 300 sunny days a year.
Washington state's most populous county includes Seattle, Bellevue, and Redmond — home to Microsoft's headquarters.
This Texas county surrounding Fort Worth is the second most populous in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex.
Iowa's capital and largest city sits at the confluence of the Raccoon and Des Moines rivers.
Home to the Alamo and the River Walk, this Texas city is the nation's seventh largest.
This Maine park on Mount Desert Island has the first sunrise in the continental US each morning.
At 1,776 feet, this glass-and-steel tower in Lower Manhattan is the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere.
Perched on seven hills by the bay, this city is famous for cable cars and sourdough bread.
This sign high above the Hollywood Hills has watched over Los Angeles since 1923 — it originally read 'HOLLYWOODLAND'.
The hottest and driest national park in North America sits below sea level in eastern California.
Michigan's most populous county encompasses Detroit and the older suburbs to its east and south.
Montana's capital city in the Rocky Mountain foothills was founded during the gold rush era.
This active volcanic park on Hawaii's Big Island features two of the world's most active volcanoes.
This Ohio city on Lake Erie was once called the Mistake on the Lake but is home to the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.
This North Dakota capital sits on the Missouri River and is named after German Chancellor Otto von Bismarck.
The nation's founding documents were signed in this Philadelphia hall, now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
This Southern California county stretches from the San Bernardino Mountains to the Salton Sea.
Plummeting 167 feet on the US-Canada border, these falls are the most powerful in North America.
Home to Apple, Google, and Meta headquarters, this Bay Area county is the heart of Silicon Valley.
The Mile High City sits exactly 5,280 feet above sea level at the foot of the Rockies.
Nebraska's capital city is home to the University of Nebraska and Husker football.
Florida's only national park covering the southern tip of the Everglades protects the largest tropical wilderness in the US.
Nevada's capital sits in the Carson Valley east of the Sierra Nevada and is named after frontiersman Kit Carson.
This Oregon park features the deepest lake in the US, formed in a collapsed volcano caldera, known for its vivid blue color.
Elvis Presley's colonial-revival mansion in Memphis became a museum after his death in 1977.
This city on Puget Sound is the birthplace of Starbucks, Amazon, and grunge music.
This North Carolina county is home to Charlotte, the state's largest city and a major banking hub.
Tennessee's most populous county encompasses Memphis and sits on the Mississippi River.
New Hampshire's capital on the Merrimack River is home to Saint Anselm College and the first primary in presidential races.
Home to the Gateway Arch and once called the Gateway to the West, this Missouri city straddles the Mississippi.
Washington state's highest peak, a glaciated stratovolcano, is surrounded by old-growth forests in this park.
This Florida complex launched every US crewed moon mission; its Vehicle Assembly Building is one of the largest structures in the world.
The Motor City, birthplace of the American automobile industry and Motown Records.
Attacked on December 7, 1941, this Pearl Harbor battleship memorial straddles the sunken USS Arizona.
Home to the world's tallest trees, this Northern California park protects ancient coastal redwood forests.
This Alabama county contains Birmingham, the state's largest city, and has a legacy tied to the civil rights movement.
The capital of New Jersey sits across the Delaware River from Philadelphia.
This park in the Chihuahuan Desert of New Mexico features the world's largest known cave room.
This city sits at the confluence of the Ohio, Allegheny, and Monongahela rivers and is known as the Steel City.
This New York capital on the Hudson River has been the state capital since 1797.
Built on a rocky island in San Francisco Bay, this former maximum-security federal penitentiary housed Al Capone.
The most densely populated county in the US is the island of Manhattan in New York City.
This 110-story Chicago skyscraper, now known as Willis Tower, was the world's tallest building from 1973 to 1998.
This Ohio county on Lake Erie contains Cleveland and was once an industrial powerhouse of the Rust Belt.
Nicknamed the Rose City, it sits near Mount Hood in the Pacific Northwest.
Oregon's capital sits in the Willamette Valley between Portland and Eugene.
The most remote national park in the US sits in the heart of the Brooks Range in northern Alaska.
rounds.answer: Gates of the Arctic National Park, AK

rounds.clue: The capital of Washington state sits at the southern end of Puget Sound.
This South Dakota park's rugged terrain reveals fossils of prehistoric animals and colorful badlands formations.

Florida's second most populous county, home to Fort Lauderdale and many Miami-area communities.

Home to the capital of Texas, this county also encompasses the University of Texas at Austin.
Wisconsin's capital is home to the University of Wisconsin and sits on an isthmus between two lakes.
A dormant volcano on Maui, this park features the world's largest dormant volcanic crater.

Built in 1914, this Chicago ballpark is famous for its ivy-covered brick outfield walls.
This Georgia county containing Atlanta is one of the fastest-growing in the Southeast.

This Virginia county adjacent to Washington, DC, is home to the Pentagon and Crystal City.

This Indiana city hosts the world's most famous auto race every Memorial Day weekend.
Rising 555 feet above the National Mall, this obelisk is the world's tallest stone structure.
Located on Lake Superior, this Michigan island park is accessible only by boat or floatplane and is car-free.
The most populous county in the US, home to Los Angeles and Hollywood.
Maine's capital city on the Kennebec River is the largest city east of the Mississippi without a direct interstate connection.
Colorado's newest national park features towering sand dunes against the backdrop of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains.

This Maryland city on the Chesapeake Bay is known for its crabs, the Inner Harbor, and Johns Hopkins University.
The capital of Maryland sits on the Severn River and is home to the US Naval Academy.
This Colorado park offers 14,000-foot peaks, alpine meadows, and the highest paved road in the US.
This Kentucky capital is located on the Kentucky River and is famous for bourbon whiskey production.
The rainiest national park in the Lower 48, this Washington park on the Olympic Peninsula features temperate rainforests.
At 180 feet tall, this St. Louis arch is the world's tallest man-made monument and symbolizes westward expansion.
Kentucky's largest city sits on the Ohio River and is famous for the Kentucky Derby and Louisville Slugger bats.
Florida's second most populous county, home to Fort Lauderdale and many Miami-area communities.
This San Francisco Bay Area county is home to Oakland, Berkeley, and the University of California's flagship campus.
Iowa's capital and largest city sits at the confluence of the Raccoon and Des Moines rivers.
The Crescent City — birthplace of jazz — sits at the mouth of the Mississippi River.
This Maine park on Mount Desert Island has the first sunrise in the continental US each morning.
Four presidents are carved into the granite face of South Dakota's Black Hills.
New Mexico's largest city sits in the Rio Grande valley and is known for its hot air balloon festival.
This Fenway Park landmark, the world's oldest professional baseball stadium, has been home to the Red Sox since 1912.
The hottest and driest national park in North America sits below sea level in eastern California.
Home to the capital of Texas, this county also encompasses the University of Texas at Austin.
Montana's capital city in the Rocky Mountain foothills was founded during the gold rush era.
This active volcanic park on Hawaii's Big Island features two of the world's most active volcanoes.
This Kansas city on the Missouri border is famous for jazz, BBQ, and the Country Club Plaza.
This North Dakota capital sits on the Missouri River and is named after German Chancellor Otto von Bismarck.
Built in 1914, this Chicago ballpark is famous for its ivy-covered brick outfield walls.
This Georgia county containing Atlanta is one of the fastest-growing in the Southeast.
This famous New Orleans street in the French Quarter is known for its Mardi Gras celebrations and vibrant nightlife.
Maryland's most populous county borders Washington, DC, and is home to many federal workers.
Nebraska's largest city on the Missouri River is the hometown of Warren Buffett.
Nebraska's capital city is home to the University of Nebraska and Husker football.
Florida's only national park covering the southern tip of the Everglades protects the largest tropical wilderness in the US.
Nevada's capital sits in the Carson Valley east of the Sierra Nevada and is named after frontiersman Kit Carson.
This Oregon park features the deepest lake in the US, formed in a collapsed volcano caldera, known for its vivid blue color.
The crossroads of the world — this iconic intersection in Midtown Manhattan is famous for its billboards and New Year's Eve ball drop.
This city at the southern tip of Manhattan is the most densely populated in the US.
This Virginia county adjacent to Washington, DC, is home to the Pentagon and Crystal City.
Colorado's most populous county contains Denver and is one of the fastest-growing large counties in the US.
New Hampshire's capital on the Merrimack River is home to Saint Anselm College and the first primary in presidential races.
The Big Easy's neighbor, this city sits where the Red River meets the Mississippi and is Louisiana's capital.
Washington state's highest peak, a glaciated stratovolcano, is surrounded by old-growth forests in this park.
This massive five-sided building in Arlington, Virginia is the headquarters of the US Department of Defense.
Wisconsin's largest city on Lake Michigan is known for Miller beer and the Harley-Davidson Museum.
Rising 555 feet above the National Mall, this obelisk is the world's tallest stone structure.
Home to the world's tallest trees, this Northern California park protects ancient coastal redwood forests.
The most populous county in the US, home to Los Angeles and Hollywood.
The capital of New Jersey sits across the Delaware River from Philadelphia.
This park in the Chihuahuan Desert of New Mexico features the world's largest known cave room.
This city in the heart of Oklahoma was largely rebuilt after a devastating 1995 federal building bombing.
This New York capital on the Hudson River has been the state capital since 1797.
This copper statue given by France stands on Liberty Island in New York Harbor.
Illinois' most populous county encompasses Chicago and its inner suburbs.
Spanning the Golden Gate strait, this iconic suspension bridge in San Francisco is painted International Orange.
Arizona's most populous county contains Phoenix, Scottsdale, and Tempe.
Virginia's capital along the James River served as the capital of the Confederacy.
Oregon's capital sits in the Willamette Valley between Portland and Eugene.
The most remote national park in the US sits in the heart of the Brooks Range in northern Alaska.
rounds.answer: Gates of the Arctic National Park, AK

rounds.clue: The capital of Washington state sits at the southern end of Puget Sound.
This South Dakota park's rugged terrain reveals fossils of prehistoric animals and colorful badlands formations.
Abraham Lincoln sits enthroned at the west end of the National Mall in Washington, DC.
This Utah city sits near the Great Salt Lake and is headquarters to the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints.
This California county on the Pacific coast is home to San Diego, the nation's eighth largest city.
Home to Disneyland and Anaheim, this Southern California county is the second most populous in the state.
West Virginia's capital and largest city sits at the confluence of the Elk and Kanawha rivers.
Tucked in the Santa Cruz Mountains, this California city is the tech hub of Silicon Valley.
Named after the 26th president, this North Dakota park protects the rugged badlands of the Little Missouri River valley.
This 102-story Art Deco skyscraper on Fifth Avenue was the world's tallest building from 1931 to 1970.
The birthplace of Elvis Presley and home to Graceland, this Tennessee city sits on the Mississippi River.
Atop Queen Anne Hill in Seattle, this 605-foot tower was built for the 1962 World's Fair.
This Colorado park contains the largest concentration of ancient Ancestral Puebloan cliff dwellings in North America.
This Florida county is the most populous in the state, home to Miami and its beaches.
Wisconsin's capital is home to the University of Wisconsin and sits on an isthmus between two lakes.
A dormant volcano on Maui, this park features the world's largest dormant volcanic crater.
This Georgia city hosted the 1996 Summer Olympics and is headquarters to Coca-Cola and CNN.
Pennsylvania's capital on the Susquehanna River has been the state capital since 1812.
Built in the Nevada desert during the Great Depression, this concrete arch-gravity dam on the Colorado River was the world's tallest when completed.
The most populous county in Texas encompasses Dallas and many of its suburbs.
This cracked iron bell in Philadelphia rang to summon lawmakers to hear the first public reading of the Declaration of Independence.
This Nevada county in the Mojave Desert is home to Las Vegas and over 2 million people.
Nicknamed the City of Brotherly Love, it's home to the Liberty Bell and Independence Hall.
The capital of South Carolina sits in the center of the state along the Congaree River.
This Texas park in the Chihuahuan Desert features the Rio Grande, hot springs, and the Chisos Mountains.
Mississippi's capital and largest city sits on the Pearl River in the western part of the state.
Kentucky's most visited attraction features the world's longest known cave system with over 400 miles of passageways.
Built in 1718, this Spanish mission in San Antonio is famous for the 1836 battle where Davy Crockett fell.
This Massachusetts city is home to the Red Sox, Harvard, and MIT.
Washington state's most populous county includes Seattle, Bellevue, and Redmond — home to Microsoft's headquarters.
This Texas county surrounding Fort Worth is the second most populous in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex.
Missouri's capital sits on the Missouri River midway between St. Louis and Kansas City.
Sin City sits in the Mojave Desert and is famous for its casinos and the Strip.
This Arkansas park was the first land set aside by the US government for public use, known for its thermal springs.
At 1,776 feet, this glass-and-steel tower in Lower Manhattan is the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere.
The Music City, home to the Grand Ole Opry and country music's biggest stars.
This sign high above the Hollywood Hills has watched over Los Angeles since 1923 — it originally read 'HOLLYWOODLAND'.
Located on Lake Superior, this Michigan island park is accessible only by boat or floatplane and is car-free.
Michigan's most populous county encompasses Detroit and the older suburbs to its east and south.
Maine's capital city on the Kennebec River is the largest city east of the Mississippi without a direct interstate connection.
Colorado's newest national park features towering sand dunes against the backdrop of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains.
rounds.answer: Great Sand Dunes National Park, CO

rounds.clue: This Texas city is the largest in the US by area and home to NASA's Johnson Space Center.
Michigan's capital city, not Detroit, is home to Michigan State University.
The nation's founding documents were signed in this Philadelphia hall, now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
This Southern California county stretches from the San Bernardino Mountains to the Salton Sea.
Plummeting 167 feet on the US-Canada border, these falls are the most powerful in North America.
Home to Apple, Google, and Meta headquarters, this Bay Area county is the heart of Silicon Valley.
Nicknamed the City of Angels, it's the entertainment capital of the world.
The Twin Cities' less-famous half, this city is actually Minnesota's capital.
This Nevada park is one of the darkest night-sky locations in the US and contains Lehman Caves.
Alabama's capital city was the first capital of the Confederacy in 1861.
South Carolina's least-visited national park protects the largest intact expanse of old-growth bottomland hardwood forest in the Southeast.
Elvis Presley's colonial-revival mansion in Memphis became a museum after his death in 1977.
This Arizona city is the most populous state capital in the US and gets over 300 sunny days a year.
This North Carolina county is home to Charlotte, the state's largest city and a major banking hub.
Tennessee's most populous county encompasses Memphis and sits on the Mississippi River.
The capital of Kansas sits on the Kansas River and is the geographic center of the contiguous US.
Home to the Alamo and the River Walk, this Texas city is the nation's seventh largest.
This Ohio park along the Cuyahoga River protects a green corridor between Cleveland and Akron.
This Florida complex launched every US crewed moon mission; its Vehicle Assembly Building is one of the largest structures in the world.
